sections.heading: Data Persistence Overview
sections.content: NEXUS stores all state and calibration matrices locally. System configuration is managed via a JSON schema, while heavy calibration outputs (homography, intrinsic/extrinsic matrices) are saved as standard NumPy archives (.npz) for fast loading.

sections.heading: System & Model Files
rows: Stores all CONFIG keys (Camera indices, model paths, hardware thresholds, PAN_TRIM/TILT_TRIM)
Compiled GPU-specific model. Not portable; an engine built on an RTX 3080 cannot run on an RTX 4090
Raw model weights. Portable across machines, but strictly used as a slow CPU fallback

sections.heading: Calibration Archives (.npz)
rows: K1, D1, K2, D2, R, T, E, F, mapL1, mapL2, mapR1, mapR2, focal_px, baseline_cm, rms
MATLAB precision matrices (identical keys to Python calibration)
H (3x3 float64 Homography), src_points, dst_points, point_count, reprojection_error

sections.heading: File Fallback & Corruption Logic
sections.alertContent: If settings.json is corrupted (JSONDecodeError) or deleted, the system regenerates it on startup with safe default values. If calibration files like stereo_calibration_py.npz are missing, the VisionEngine degrades gracefully to '⚠️ UNCALIBRATED RAW' mode without throwing an exception, though depth estimation will be disabled.
